title: Live Vercel API token stored in plaintext in .env.local
impact: Token grants Vercel API access; any workspace snapshot, artifact archive, container image, or shared dev environment leaks a live credential. Token is not used anywhere in source code.
evidence: HELIX_VERCEL_TOKEN=vcp_1Nb4zesMHVDtft3qtcWtGXUeTs7sE2nwzqYKiYcmbYg2FQ37a52szwxD on line 10
fix_suggestion: Rotate token immediately in Vercel dashboard. Remove line from .env.local.

title: Zero authentication on all 9 API routes
impact: Any unauthenticated HTTP request can read issue data, trigger child process spawning, or mutate persistent JSON on disk. Healthcare vulnerability tracking data fully exposed.
evidence: No middleware.ts exists in apps/admin/src. Every route handler executes with zero session or token check. No auth library (next-auth, clerk, etc.) in package.json.
fix_suggestion: Create apps/admin/src/middleware.ts gating all /api/* routes with session or Authorization header validation before any handler executes.

title: Unauthenticated remote process execution via POST /api/tests/run
impact: Any unauthenticated caller in a non-production environment can spawn a full Playwright/Chromium test run, exhausting CPU/memory and enabling DoS. NODE_ENV guard is easily bypassed in staging/preview.
evidence: spawn('npx', ['vitest', 'run', filePath]) with no auth check. Only guard is process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production' at line 32.
fix_suggestion: Add authentication middleware. Replace NODE_ENV guard with explicit ENABLE_TEST_RUNNER=true opt-in flag. Auth check must precede the flag check.

title: Path traversal vulnerability in /api/reviews/[date]
impact: Attacker can read arbitrary .json files on the server by encoding ../ sequences in the date URL segment. join(REVIEWS_DIR, date+'.json') does not sanitize path traversal sequences.
evidence: const reviewPath = join(REVIEWS_DIR, `${date}.json`); — no validation that date matches YYYY-MM-DD format before path construction.
fix_suggestion: Validate date against /^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$/ before joining. Assert reviewPath.startsWith(REVIEWS_DIR + '/') as defense in depth.

title: No security headers configured on admin application
impact: No CSP, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, HSTS, Referrer-Policy, or Permissions-Policy. Dashboard is clickjackable, MIME sniffing enabled, any XSS has no browser-enforced containment.
evidence: next.config.ts contains only transpilePackages: ['@helix/tokens']. vercel.json sets only X-Robots-Tag noindex.
fix_suggestion: Add headers() function to next.config.ts with X-Frame-Options: DENY, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, Strict-Transport-Security, Referrer-Policy, and a baseline Content-Security-Policy.

title: No test framework installed or configured in admin app
impact: Zero test files exist. CI quality gate requiring 80%+ coverage is completely unenforceable. Bad deployments cannot be blocked by automated tests for the application that gates healthcare component releases.
evidence: find apps/admin/src -name '*.test.*' returns nothing. No vitest, jest, @testing-library/react, or test runner in devDependencies. No test script in package.json.
fix_suggestion: Install Vitest (consistent with library package), @testing-library/react, happy-dom, and msw. Add test and test:coverage scripts. Configure vitest.config.ts with 80% coverage thresholds.

title: calculateGrade() has zero test coverage — controls component release decisions
impact: calculateGrade() directly determines A/B/C/D/F grades deciding if components ship to patient-facing healthcare systems. Five grade paths, three penalty multiplier branches, 17 dimension inputs — zero tests verify any of them. A bug could silently allow an inaccessible component to receive grade A.
evidence: No corresponding *.test.ts file exists. The zero-score penalty at lines 209-228 caps grade at C — unverified by any test.
fix_suggestion: Create apps/admin/src/lib/__tests__/health-scorer.test.ts covering all grade paths, boundary conditions at each threshold, and all penalty edge cases.

title: computeStats() / loadIssues() / saveIssues() have zero test coverage
impact: computeStats() is the source of truth for all dashboard issue statistics. saveIssues() overwrites the issues database on every mutation. loadIssues() calls readFileSync with no try/catch — corrupt disk state crashes all API routes with a permanent 500 error.
evidence: No test file. resolutionRate uses Math.round(...* 10000) / 10000 — one typo inverts the displayed percentage by 10x. saveIssues silently mutates its input argument.
fix_suggestion: Create apps/admin/src/lib/__tests__/issues-loader.test.ts with mocked fs, covering empty array edge cases, stat precision, and save mutation side effects.

title: All 9 API routes have zero test coverage
impact: Critical bugs exist undetected: (1) POST /api/issues ID generation creates collisions when an issue is deleted and recreated with the same prefix. (2) PATCH resolvedAt clear logic reads old status before updating — correct but fragile. (3) Production guard in test runner is untested.
evidence: Zero test files under apps/admin/src/app/api/. ID collision: existingWithPrefix.length + 1 reuses IDs after deletion.
fix_suggestion: Route handler tests for each route covering happy path, 400 for invalid input, 404 for unknown IDs, 500 for mocked filesystem failures, and filter/sort correctness.

title: No skip navigation link — keyboard users must tab through 10 sidebar items on every page
impact: Keyboard and screen reader users must tab through all 10 sidebar navigation links before reaching page content on every page load. Violates WCAG 2.4.1 Bypass Blocks (Level A, prerequisite for AA compliance).
evidence: <aside> with 10 nav items rendered before <main>. No skip link anywhere in the rendered HTML.
fix_suggestion:
Add Skip to main content before the sidebar. Add id='main-content' to
.

title: GlassIssueCard interactive div is not keyboard accessible
impact: Every issue card in the roadmap grid (the primary interactive surface) uses a <div onClick> with no role, tabindex, or keyboard handler. Keyboard-only users cannot reach or activate any issue card. WCAG 4.1.2 and 2.1.1 violations.
evidence: <div className='... cursor-pointer ...' onClick={onClick}> — cursor-pointer is CSS cosmetic only, grants no accessibility semantics.
fix_suggestion: Replace outer <div> with <button type='button' className='... w-full text-left' aria-label={`View details for issue: ${issue.title}`} onClick={onClick}>.

title: IssueDetailPanel missing role=dialog, aria-modal, and focus management
impact: Slide-out panel is a modal experience but has no role=dialog, aria-modal=true, or aria-labelledby. Focus does not move to the panel on open. Screen readers continue reading background content. Close button at line 165 has no aria-label. WCAG 2.4.3, 2.1.2, 4.1.2 violations.
evidence: <div className='fixed inset-y-0 right-0 w-[500px] ...'> with no role attribute. <button onClick={onClose}><X /></button> with no aria-label.
fix_suggestion: Add role='dialog' aria-modal='true' aria-labelledby='issue-detail-title' to panel. Add aria-label='Close issue detail panel' to close button. Use useEffect to move focus on open and return focus on close.

title: Heatmap dimension buttons have no accessible names
impact: Each heatmap cell is a <button> identified only by a title attribute. title is not reliably announced by NVDA and is invisible to touch users. The entire heatmap is inaccessible to assistive technology. WCAG 4.1.2 and 1.1.1 violations.
evidence: <button ... title={`${component.tagName} - ${dimension.name}: ...`} /> — no aria-label attribute.
fix_suggestion: Replace title attribute with aria-label using the same content string.

title: CodeSnippets tabs missing ARIA tab role pattern
impact: Tab bar has no role=tablist. Buttons have no role=tab or aria-selected. Panel has no role=tabpanel. Screen readers announce anonymous buttons with no selection state. WCAG 4.1.2 violation. The legacy CodeSnippetPanel.tsx implements this correctly — the newer component regressed.
evidence: <div className='flex items-center gap-1'> container + <button onClick={() => setActiveTab(...)}>  — no ARIA roles anywhere.
fix_suggestion: Add role=tablist to container, role=tab aria-selected={isActive} aria-controls={...} to each button, role=tabpanel aria-labelledby={...} to content div.

title: SeverityTabs buttons missing ARIA tab roles and selected state
impact: No role=tablist, no role=tab, no aria-selected. Active state conveyed only through visual styling — invisible to screen readers. WCAG 4.1.2 violation.
evidence: <div className='flex items-center gap-2 ...'> + <button onClick={() => onTabChange(tab.value)}> — no ARIA roles.
fix_suggestion: Add role=tablist aria-label='Filter by severity' to container; role=tab aria-selected={isActive} to each button.

title: Blocking writeFileSync on every Health page render stalls Node.js event loop
impact: saveHealthSnapshot() calls writeFileSync synchronously on every GET request to /health. Stalls the event loop, blocks all concurrent requests, creates race conditions under load, makes the page non-idempotent and non-cacheable. File comment itself acknowledges: 'In production, this would be triggered by a cron job.'
evidence: saveHealthSnapshot(components); directly in async function HealthPage() body. writeFileSync(filePath, ...) in health-history-writer.ts:84.
fix_suggestion: Remove saveHealthSnapshot from the page. Create POST /api/health/snapshot triggered by cron or CI. The Server Component should only read data.

title: Unsanitized sort parameter used as object property accessor
impact: User-supplied ?sort= value cast to Record<string,unknown> for bracket-notation access. ?sort=__proto__ probes prototype properties. Any field including internal ones (statusHistory, notes) can be sorted.
evidence: const aRecord = a as unknown as Record<string, unknown>; const aVal = String(aRecord[sort] ?? '');
fix_suggestion: Validate against explicit allowlist: const SORTABLE = new Set(['createdAt', 'severity', 'status', 'updatedAt', 'category']);

title: Error responses expose internal filesystem paths via detail field
impact: error.message from readFileSync includes absolute paths (e.g. ENOENT: no such file, open '/workspace/.claude/issues/issues.json'). Reveals server filesystem layout to unauthenticated callers.
evidence: return NextResponse.json({ error: 'Failed to load issues', detail: message }, { status: 500 });
fix_suggestion: Only include detail in development: ...(process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development' && { detail: message })

title: No runtime type validation on POST /api/issues — accepts arbitrary strings and unlimited lengths
impact: Presence check only. Attacker can POST severity='anything', title='A'.repeat(100000) (disk exhaustion), tags=[1,2,3] (type corruption in stored JSON database).
evidence: const body = (await request.json()) as CreateIssueBody; — if (!body.title || !body.severity) is the only validation.
fix_suggestion: Add Zod schema or manual validation enforcing valid enum values and string length limits for all fields.

title: PATCH /api/issues/[id] has no status enum validation or notes length cap
impact: Any string accepted as status, corrupting byStatus statistics. Unbounded note appends allow disk exhaustion via repeated PATCH requests with no rate limiting.
evidence: if (body.status !== undefined && body.status !== issue.status) { issue.status = body.status; } — no enum check.
fix_suggestion: Validate body.status against VALID_STATUSES set. Cap body.notes at 1000 chars with a length check.

title: Child process spawn error messages sent verbatim to client
impact: System-level spawn errors containing server paths and command strings sent in SSE events to unauthenticated callers.
evidence: child.on('error', (err) => { send({ error: err.message }) }) — raw system error message exposed.
fix_suggestion: Replace with sanitized message: error: 'Test runner process failed to start.'

title: URL construction from env vars without protocol validation — open redirect surface
impact: DOCS_URL and STORYBOOK_URL used in href attributes and getStorybookUrl(tag) interpolates CEM tag names. A maliciously crafted CEM file or env var set to javascript:alert(1) creates an XSS vector.
evidence: export const DOCS_URL = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_HELIX_DOCS_URL || 'http://localhost:3150'; — no protocol validation.
fix_suggestion: Validate with new URL(val) and assert protocol is http: or https:. Validate tag against hx-[a-z-]+ before interpolating into URLs.

title: No rate limiting or concurrency guard on CPU-intensive test runner endpoint
impact: Each invocation spawns multiple Playwright/Chromium processes, holds SSE connections for up to 300s, writes/reads files. Concurrent requests exhaust CPU, memory, and file descriptors.
evidence: No concurrency check before spawn(). No 429 response for concurrent calls. maxDuration=300 in Vercel config.
fix_suggestion: Add module-level testRunInProgress flag returning 429 if already running. Add authentication (see CRITICAL finding).

title: Stale --wc- token prefix silently deflates all component accessibility scores since commit c643de6
impact: Every component has been failing the Token-based colors accessibility check since the --wc- to --hx- migration. All accessibility dimension scores are systematically understated, producing false urgency and wrong grade data for every component.
evidence: const usesTokenColors = combined.includes('--wc-color-') || combined.includes('var(--wc-'); — prefix should be --hx- after commit c643de6.
fix_suggestion: Immediately update to --hx-color- and var(--hx-. Add regression tests for classifyTest() including the startsWith('form') prefix false-positive bug.

title: No custom 404 or 500 error pages — default Next.js errors break layout shell
impact: notFound() is called in [tag]/page.tsx:43 but no not-found.tsx exists. Default Next.js 404 renders outside the admin sidebar layout. No error.tsx means runtime errors from file I/O break outside the shell entirely.
evidence: find apps/admin/src/app -name 'not-found.tsx' -o -name 'error.tsx' returns nothing.
fix_suggestion: Create apps/admin/src/app/not-found.tsx and apps/admin/src/app/error.tsx rendering within the dashboard layout shell.

title: GitHub Actions pinned to mutable tags — supply chain risk
impact: Mutable tags (actions/checkout@v4, actions/setup-node@v4) can be moved to point to malicious code without notice. Supply-chain attack vector for a healthcare enterprise library.
evidence: uses: actions/checkout@v4 — mutable tag, not an immutable commit SHA. Same pattern in ci-matrix.yml:41.
fix_suggestion: Pin all actions to full commit SHAs: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683 # v4.2.2

title: No automated release/publish workflow
impact: Changesets configured but no workflow triggers changeset publish. Library versions must be published manually with no post-publish CDN invalidation or docs deployment.
evidence: Only ci.yml and ci-matrix.yml exist in .github/workflows/. No release.yml or publish.yml.
fix_suggestion: Create .github/workflows/release.yml using changesets/action to automate version PRs and npm publish on merge to main.

title: scoreAllComponents() called 3x independently with no React cache()
impact: Each page independently runs the full 17-analyzer pipeline across all components on every request. Three separate page visits re-run the entire analysis independently with no shared cache.
evidence: const healthScores = await scoreAllComponents(); — identical call in page.tsx:36, components/page.tsx:11, and health/page.tsx:14 with no cache() wrapper.
fix_suggestion: Wrap scoreAllComponents with React cache(): export const scoreAllComponents = cache(async () => { ... }). Add export const revalidate = 60 to consuming pages.

title: lucide-react barrel imports without optimizePackageImports config
impact: lucide-react has 500+ icons. Without experimental.optimizePackageImports, entire barrel evaluated at module resolution time. 35+ unique icons spread across layout and page files.
evidence: next.config.ts has no experimental key. All imports are named barrel imports from 'lucide-react'.
fix_suggestion: Add to next.config.ts: experimental: { optimizePackageImports: ['lucide-react', 'recharts', 'shiki'] }

title: Recharts loaded in initial bundle without dynamic import or Suspense boundary
impact: Recharts (~300KB minified) in the initial dashboard bundle. Chart components have no dynamic() import and no Suspense boundary. Charts animate on hydration causing visible layout shift.
evidence: 'use client'; import { PieChart, Pie, Cell } from 'recharts'; — imported directly in page.tsx with no dynamic wrapper.
fix_suggestion: const TestCategoryDonut = dynamic(() => import('@/components/dashboard/TestCategoryDonut'), { ssr: false, loading: () => <ChartSkeleton /> })

title: Tokens layout entirely client-side due to usePathname in layout component
impact: 'use client' on the tokens layout forces all 7 token sub-pages into the client bundle. Server Component streaming and static generation benefits lost for entire /tokens/* subtree.
evidence: 'use client'; import { usePathname } from 'next/navigation'; — entire layout is a client component just for active-state styling.
fix_suggestion: Extract a <TokenNavLink> client component for the usePathname active-state check. Keep TokensLayout as a Server Component.

title: HealthDashboard is an unnecessary client component pulling entire subtree client-side
impact: Entire health dashboard subtree (TrendChart, TeamLeaderboard, Heatmap, ComponentDrillDown) bundled for the client because HealthDashboard uses 'use client' solely for one useState.
evidence: 'use client'; — only client-side requirement is useState<ComponentHealth | null> for the drill-down panel.
fix_suggestion: Extract selected-component state into a small client wrapper. Keep HealthDashboard and static sub-components as Server Components.

title: No loading.tsx files anywhere — no streaming or Suspense fallbacks
impact: Health page runs 17-analyzer scoreAllComponents(). Component detail page initializes Shiki WASM. Both show completely blank pages while loading with no skeleton, spinner, or progress indicator.
evidence: find apps/admin/src/app -name 'loading.tsx' returns nothing.
fix_suggestion: Create loading.tsx in src/app/health/, src/app/components/[tag]/, and src/app/ root.

title: FilterBar form inputs have no labels — WCAG 1.3.1 violation
impact: Four form controls (search text, status select, category select, reporter select) have no label, aria-label, or aria-labelledby. Placeholders are not label substitutes. Screen readers announce 'edit text' and 'combo box' with no context.
evidence: <input type='text' placeholder='Search issues...' /> — no label association of any kind. All four controls share this pattern.
fix_suggestion: Add aria-label='Search issues' to input; aria-label='Filter by status' to selects. Or use <label htmlFor='...'> with id pairs.

title: HooksFilter badges used as interactive controls without keyboard support
impact: Badge renders a <div>. Filter toggles built on <div onClick> are not reachable by Tab, cannot be activated by Enter/Space. Keyboard users cannot filter the hooks list. WCAG 4.1.2 violation.
evidence: <Badge className='cursor-pointer' onClick={() => onPriorityChange(priority)}> — Badge is a div, not a button.
fix_suggestion: Replace with <button type='button' aria-pressed={isSelected}> styled as a badge.

title: TestResultsTable expand/collapse buttons missing aria-expanded
impact: Component group expand buttons show Unicode arrows but have no aria-expanded. Screen readers cannot communicate open/closed state. WCAG 4.1.2 violation.
evidence: <button onClick={() => toggle(group.component)}> — no aria-expanded attribute. Unicode arrows ▼/▶ not aria-hidden.
fix_suggestion: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to button. Add aria-hidden='true' to the arrow span.

title: SplitButtonDropdown missing arrow key keyboard navigation for menu items
impact: Component implements role=menu and role=menuitem correctly but omits all WAI-ARIA Menu Button keyboard interactions. Arrow Down/Up navigation, Home/End, Tab to close — none implemented. Focus stays on trigger when menu opens.
evidence: No onKeyDown handler on the menu container or items for arrow key navigation.
fix_suggestion: Add onKeyDown implementing Arrow Up/Down item focus navigation, Escape to close.

title: CSS animations have no prefers-reduced-motion media query
impact: Five continuous animations (bar-shimmer, bar-warn, bar-fail, bar-coverage, indeterminate-bar) on progress bars run with no prefers-reduced-motion override. Users with vestibular disorders affected. WCAG 2.3.1 concern.
evidence: animation: bar-shimmer 3s ease-in-out infinite; — no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) wrapper anywhere in globals.css.
fix_suggestion: Add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .bar-pass, .bar-warn, .bar-fail, .bar-coverage { animation: none; } }

title: Zero per-page metadata exports across 10 sub-pages
impact: Every page displays the same root layout title. Engineers cannot distinguish open tabs. Dynamic [tag] route has no generateMetadata function preventing per-component titles.
evidence: None of the 10 sub-pages (health, components, tests, pipeline, roadmap, hooks, tokens, tokens/colors, components/[tag]) export metadata or generateMetadata.
fix_suggestion: Add export const metadata: Metadata = { title: '[PageName] — Panopticon Platform' } to each page. Add generateMetadata to [tag]/page.tsx.

title: Missing favicon — blank browser tab icon for always-open internal dashboard
impact: No favicon.ico, icon.png, or apple-icon.png exists anywhere in the project. Browser tabs show blank icon for an internal dashboard engineers keep permanently open.
evidence: ls apps/admin/public/ returns only robots.txt.
fix_suggestion: Place icon.ico or icon.png in apps/admin/src/app/ for Next.js 15 app-router auto-detection.

title: Hardcoded date literal 2026-02-15 used for production review data loading
impact: When a new review file is written (e.g. 2026-03-01.json), the roadmap page silently continues loading stale 2026-02-15 data with no error or warning. Silent correctness failure.
evidence: const reviewPath = join(process.cwd(), '../../.claude/issues/reviews/2026-02-15.json'); — and reviewDate='2026-02-15' at line 50. loadReviewsIndex() already exists to get this dynamically.
fix_suggestion: Replace hardcoded date with dynamic lookup via loadReviewsIndex() from issues-loader.ts.

title: as unknown as double-cast bypasses type system in API sort handler
impact: Canonical escape hatch defeating all type safety. 'a' is already typed as TrackedIssue. Double-cast papers over a design gap instead of fixing it. Enables arbitrary property access on typed objects.
evidence: const aRecord = a as unknown as Record<string, unknown>; const aVal = String(aRecord[sort] ?? '');
fix_suggestion: Define type TrackedIssueSortKey = keyof TrackedIssue and validate sort against a Set<string> of explicitly allowed field names.

title: getLibraryRoot() function duplicated across 14 analyzer files
impact: Identical function body in 14 separate files. If the monorepo layout changes, all 14 must be updated. Dead _getLibraryRoot() in health-scorer.ts:65 proves divergence has already started.
evidence: grep -r 'function getLibraryRoot' apps/admin/src/lib returns 14+ identical implementations.
fix_suggestion: Create apps/admin/src/lib/paths.ts, export a single getLibraryRoot(), import from there in all analyzers.

title: loadIssues() can throw synchronously in Server Component with no error boundary
impact: loadIssues() calls readFileSync with no try/catch. A missing or corrupt issues.json during a cold deploy crashes the entire roadmap page, returning an unhandled 500 to all users indefinitely.
evidence: export default function RoadmapPage() { const issuesData = loadIssues(); — bare call with no error handling.
fix_suggestion: Wrap in try/catch and render an error state, or add error handling inside loadIssues().

title: Floating promise from async handleStatusChange in synchronous callback
impact: handleStatusChange('complete') called without await or void in a synchronous callback. The async call's rejection is silently swallowed — errors on issue status updates go unreported to users and to error monitoring.
evidence: const handleResolve = useCallback(() => { if (issue && ...) { handleStatusChange('complete'); } }, ...); — handleStatusChange is async.
fix_suggestion: Use void handleStatusChange('complete'); to make the intentional fire-and-forget explicit.

title: Synchronous file reads block event loop — no pagination on issues list
impact: readFileSync blocks the event loop on every GET request. Response latency grows linearly with file size, blocking all concurrent requests. No pagination — entire issue list serialized on every call.
evidence: readFileSync(issuesPath, 'utf-8') in loadIssues() called from API GET handler with no caching or async alternative.
fix_suggestion: Replace with async readFile, or add 30s in-process cache. Add limit/offset query params to GET /api/issues.

title: Turborepo remote caching not configured in CI
impact: Neither CI workflow has TURBO_TOKEN or TURBO_TEAM env vars. Every CI run rebuilds from scratch. CLAUDE.md describes remote caching as required but it is never configured.
evidence: grep for TURBO_TOKEN in ci.yml and ci-matrix.yml returns nothing.
fix_suggestion: Add TURBO_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.TURBO_TOKEN }} and TURBO_TEAM: ${{ secrets.TURBO_TEAM }} to both workflow files.

title: Node.js version not pinned to exact version in CI
impact: node-version: 20 silently adopts new minor/patch releases, creating flaky CI failures after Node.js updates that are unrelated to code changes.
evidence: node-version: 20 — major version only, not pinned.
fix_suggestion: Pin to node-version: '20.18.0'

title: @helix/tokens uses wildcard * version specifier
impact: "@helix/tokens": "*" resolves to the highest available version including future breaking majors. Non-standard pattern that bypasses the version contract.
evidence: "@helix/tokens": "*" in dependencies — should use workspace protocol.
fix_suggestion: Change to "@helix/tokens": "workspace:*" to make workspace-local resolution explicit.

title: Env var name mismatch between root and admin .env.local
impact: Root .env.local defines NEXT_PUBLIC_DOCS_URL and NEXT_PUBLIC_STORYBOOK_URL. Admin env.ts reads NEXT_PUBLIC_HELIX_DOCS_URL. Root-level vars are dead configuration — confuses new developers setting up the environment.
evidence: Root: NEXT_PUBLIC_DOCS_URL=http://localhost:3150. Admin env.ts reads: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_HELIX_DOCS_URL
fix_suggestion: Remove unused root-level env var names, or standardize on one naming scheme and update env.ts accordingly.

title: Non-functional Export CSV button has no onClick handler
impact: <button> with no onClick, no type='button'. Clicking does nothing. Appears interactive to both sighted users and assistive technology. Silent failure for all users.
evidence: <button className='inline-flex items-center ...'><Download />Export CSV</button> — no onClick attribute.
fix_suggestion: Implement CSV export or remove the button. At minimum, add disabled attribute until implementation is complete.

title: Silent API failure in handleStatusChange — no user feedback on error
impact: The catch block comment says 'Silently fail - user can retry.' The user does not know they need to — the spinner disappears and the status reverts with no indication the PATCH request failed.
evidence: } catch { // Silently fail - user can retry } — empty catch block.
fix_suggestion: Add local error state; display error message adjacent to the status select on failure. Clear on next attempt.

title: TestRunnerPanel has 12 useState calls — extract useTestRunner hook
impact: 12 tightly coupled useState calls for streaming run state. Multiple sequential setX() calls in runTests cause cascading re-renders. Violates the project's max-3-state convention by 4x.
evidence: Lines 50-66: isRunning, results, passed, failed, elapsed, completed, finalResults, currentComponent, completedFiles, totalFiles, componentList, flavorIndex — 12 separate useState calls.
fix_suggestion: Extract a useTestRunner(initialResults) hook managing state via useReducer.

title: app/page.tsx is 993 lines — 5x the 200-line guideline
impact: Dashboard page contains multiple embedded components, inline chart markup, inline SVG icons, and confidence bar rendering logic in a single file. Maintainability severely degraded.
evidence: File length: 993 lines. Contains KpiCard, SectionCard, quality gates grid, resolution rate SVG chart, and per-component breakdown all inline.
fix_suggestion: Extract QualityGatesCard, ComponentConfidenceTable, and DimensionHeatRow as separate component files.

title: Hardcoded brand hex colors in inline styles across 12+ locations
impact: #FF4785 (Storybook) and #8B5CF6 (docs) hardcoded in at least 12 inline style occurrences across two files. No single source of truth, violates design token mandate.
evidence: style={{ color: '#FF4785' }} and style={{ background: 'linear-gradient(135deg, rgba(255,71,133,0.12) ...' }} — same values duplicated across layout.tsx and page.tsx.
fix_suggestion: Define --brand-storybook: #FF4785; --brand-docs: #8B5CF6; in globals.css and reference via CSS variables.

title: JSON.parse cast without runtime validation — CEM manifest
impact: return JSON.parse(raw) as CemManifest; — zero runtime shape validation. A CEM schema mismatch scatters undefined errors across all 9 analyzers consuming CEM data.
evidence: const raw = readFileSync(cemPath, 'utf-8'); return JSON.parse(raw) as CemManifest;
fix_suggestion: Add type guard checking schemaVersion and modules array exist before returning.

title: || used for nullish coalescing on env vars — masks empty string misconfiguration
impact: || treats empty string '' as falsy, silently falling through to localhost defaults when env vars are misconfigured to empty string. Project convention requires ??
evidence: export const DOCS_URL = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_HELIX_DOCS_URL || 'http://localhost:3150';
fix_suggestion: Replace || with ?? throughout env.ts.

title: readSource() duplicated across 7 analyzer files
impact: Identical readSource(tagName) implementation in 7 separate files. Same DRY violation as getLibraryRoot(). 7 points of failure for a change to file reading logic.
evidence: grep -r 'function readSource' apps/admin/src/lib returns 7 identical implementations.
fix_suggestion: Export readSource() from source-analyzer.ts and import from there in all 7 consumers.

title: Missing not-found.tsx — default Next.js 404 renders outside admin layout shell
impact: notFound() is called in [tag]/page.tsx:43 when a component tag is not found, but no not-found.tsx exists. Default Next.js 404 renders without the sidebar, breaking the admin UI shell.
evidence: [tag]/page.tsx:43: if (!data) notFound(); — but find apps/admin/src/app -name 'not-found.tsx' returns nothing.
fix_suggestion: Create apps/admin/src/app/not-found.tsx rendering within the dashboard layout shell.

title: Missing error.tsx — runtime errors break outside the sidebar layout
impact: Unhandled Server Component errors from file I/O in health scorer, CEM parser, or issue loader crash outside the admin layout, showing the raw Next.js error screen.
evidence: find apps/admin/src/app -name 'error.tsx' returns nothing. Multiple pages perform filesystem reads that can throw.
fix_suggestion: Create apps/admin/src/app/error.tsx as a 'use client' error boundary component rendering within the layout.

title: Duplicate h1 in sidebar brand heading and every page's main heading
impact:
Sidebar renders

Panopticon Platform

. Every page also renders its own

. Two h1 elements per page violates WCAG 2.4.6 heading hierarchy and disrupts screen reader landmark navigation.
evidence:
layout.tsx:57:

Panopticon Platform

AND page.tsx:85:

Dashboard
fix_suggestion: Change the sidebar brand heading in layout.tsx from h1 to p or span with the same visual styling.

title: ui/tabs.tsx base component missing all ARIA tab pattern roles
impact: TabsList has no role=tablist. TabsTrigger has no role=tab or aria-selected. TabsContent has no role=tabpanel. This base component is used across the application — fixing it propagates to all consumers. WCAG 4.1.2 violation.
evidence: TabsList renders <div> without role=tablist. TabsTrigger renders <button> with no role=tab or aria-selected.
fix_suggestion: Add role=tablist to TabsList, role=tab aria-selected={isActive} to TabsTrigger, role=tabpanel aria-labelledby={...} to TabsContent.

title: PipelineFlow StatusDot uses color alone to convey health status — WCAG 1.4.1 violation
impact: StatusDot renders a green or red circle with no text alternative. Used in 4 pipeline table columns (JSDoc, CEM, Story, Docs). Screen reader users receive zero information about pipeline health.
evidence: <div className={cn('w-3 h-3 rounded-full', healthy ? 'bg-emerald-400' : 'bg-red-400')} /> — no role or aria-label.
fix_suggestion: Add role='img' aria-label={healthy ? 'Healthy' : 'Needs attention'}.

title: groupByComponent called without useMemo — O(n log n) sort on every render
impact: groupByComponent(results.tests) is called on every render. Iterates all tests, builds a Map, sorts — O(n log n) on hundreds of tests, on every parent re-render.
evidence: const groups = groupByComponent(results.tests); — no useMemo wrapper.
fix_suggestion: const groups = useMemo(() => groupByComponent(results.tests), [results.tests]);

title: Token API response has new Date() on every call preventing HTTP caching
impact: generatedAt: new Date().toISOString() changes on every call. Token data is static (from a compiled package). Every response is unique, defeating all HTTP caching at CDN or browser level.
evidence: generatedAt: new Date().toISOString(), in buildTokenResponse()
fix_suggestion: Compute generatedAt once at module initialization or derive from package version.

title: force-dynamic on API routes serving static or slow-changing data
impact: Tokens route serves data from a static compiled package but opts out of all caching. Reviews route reads a JSON file changing only on deploy. Both re-execute from scratch on every request.
evidence: export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic'; in tokens/route.ts and reviews/route.ts — neither has time-based revalidation.
fix_suggestion: Remove force-dynamic from tokens route; add revalidate = 3600. Add revalidate = 60 to reviews route.

title: changeset access set to restricted — may block public publishing
impact: 'access': 'restricted' means npm publishes are private, requiring a paid npm organization. If the library is intended to be public open source, publishing will fail.
evidence: "access": "restricted" in .changeset/config.json line 6.
fix_suggestion: Confirm intent. If public, change to 'access': 'public'.

title: CI build step ordering duplicates Turborepo pipeline work
impact: Explicit npm run build at line 100 re-runs what Turborepo already executed inside the test step, wasting CI minutes on every pipeline run.
evidence: Sequential steps: type-check, lint, test, then build — Turborepo task dependencies already handle build-before-test.
fix_suggestion: Consolidate to a single turbo run lint type-check test build cem invocation.

title: Array index used as React key on mutable data in IssueDetailPanel
impact: key={idx} on tags, notes, and statusHistory — data that grows over time. Index keys cause stale state during reconciliation when items are added or removed.
evidence: issue.tags.map((tag, idx) => <Badge key={idx}>) at line 288, notes.map at 308, statusHistory.map at 328.
fix_suggestion: key={tag} for tags. key={`${change.date}-${change.from}`} for statusHistory.

title: formatLabel() duplicated across 3 files in the same roadmap directory
impact: Identical function copy-pasted into IssueDetailPanel.tsx:53, GlassIssueCard.tsx:27, and FilterBar.tsx:33. A logic change requires three simultaneous edits.
evidence: function formatLabel(str: string): string { return str.replace(/-/g, ' ').replace(/\b\w/g, (l) => l.toUpperCase()); } — identical in all three files.
fix_suggestion: Extract to apps/admin/src/app/roadmap/components/utils.ts and import from there.

title: globSync from node:fs requires Node.js 22+ — will throw on Node.js 20
impact: globSync was added to Node.js fs module in Node.js 22. Import throws at runtime on Node.js 20. No Node.js engine requirement declared in package.json.
evidence: import { globSync } from 'node:fs'; — also in test-results-reader.ts:5.
fix_suggestion: Use the glob npm package instead, or declare "engines": { "node": ">=22.0.0" } in package.json.

title: loadReview returns unknown | null — redundant union type
impact: unknown | null simplifies to unknown since null is assignable to unknown. Return type is misleading and inconsistent with the rest of the codebase using T | null.
evidence: export function loadReview(date: string): unknown | null {
fix_suggestion: Define a ReviewSnapshot interface and type accordingly, or use unknown alone.

title: Lucide icons not consistently aria-hidden across the admin application
impact: Decorative icons adjacent to visible text are not aria-hidden=true, causing screen readers to announce SVG element content. Widespread pattern across all pages and components. WCAG 1.1.1 best practice.
evidence: layout.tsx:122: <Component className='w-4 h-4 text-white' /> — no aria-hidden. Same pattern in page.tsx, health/page.tsx, and dozens of component files.
fix_suggestion: Add aria-hidden='true' to all decorative Lucide icon components where adjacent visible text provides the label.

title: Tokens layout nav links missing aria-current for active page
impact: Active token section link is visually distinguished but has no aria-current='page'. Screen readers cannot identify the current section from the navigation. WCAG 1.3.1 best practice.
evidence: isActive ? '... border-foreground ...' : '...' — visual-only active state, no aria-current.
fix_suggestion: Add aria-current={isActive ? 'page' : undefined} to each nav Link.

title: ComponentDrillDown uses emoji characters as status indicators without text alternatives
impact: 🔴 and 🟡 emojis read by screen readers as 'large red circle' and 'large yellow circle' — verbose and unexpected. ✓ may read as 'check mark'. WCAG 1.1.1 concern.
evidence: '🔴 Critical: Immediate attention required' and '🟡 Warning: Improvement recommended' as text content.
fix_suggestion: Add aria-hidden='true' to emoji spans and ensure adjacent text conveys the status independently.

title: No Open Graph or Twitter Card metadata — blank previews when sharing links
impact: When team members share admin dashboard URLs in Slack/Teams, link previews are entirely blank. Low priority for internal tool but degrades team DX.
evidence: export const metadata = { title: '...', description: '...', robots: {...} } — no openGraph or twitter fields.
fix_suggestion: Add openGraph: { title: 'Panopticon Platform', description: '...', type: 'website' } to root layout metadata.

title: ComponentCard uses window.location.href inside a Link-wrapped card
impact: window.location.href causes full browser navigation, discarding Next.js prefetching and client-side transition. The entire card is already wrapped in <Link> navigating to the same URL — the Details button is a redundant hard-navigation.
evidence: onClick={() => { window.location.href = `/components/${health.tagName}`; }} — inside a card already wrapped in <Link href={`/components/${health.tagName}`}>.
fix_suggestion: Remove the Details button (Link wrapper makes card navigable) or replace with <Link>. Replace window.open() with <a target='_blank'> elements.
